exercises.title: What happens when a consumer group reads a 3-partition topic?
exercises.scenario: Kafka assigns partitions to consumer threads in a group.
exercises.instructions:
A topic 'order-events' has 3 partitions. You start a consumer with concurrency='2':

```java
@KafkaListener(topics="order-events", groupId="g1", concurrency="2")
public void handle(OrderEvent e) { ... }
```

How are the 3 partitions distributed across 2 threads?
What if you add a third service instance (also with concurrency='2', same groupId='g1')?
What happens if you set concurrency='5' on a 3-partition topic?
exercises.expected_solution:
concurrency=2: 2 threads in group 'g1'
  Thread-1 → partitions 0 and 1
  Thread-2 → partition 2
(3 partitions ÷ 2 threads: one thread gets 2 partitions)

Second instance with concurrency=2:
  Now 4 threads in group 'g1' total (2 from each instance)
  Kafka rebalances: each of the 4 threads gets... 
  1 partition, and 1 thread sits IDLE (3 partitions < 4 threads)

concurrency=5 on 3-partition topic:
  5 threads registered, but only 3 get partitions
  2 threads are idle — WASTED resources
  Rule: concurrency ≤ partition count

test_cases.expected:
concurrency=2: 2 threads in group 'g1'
  Thread-1 → partitions 0 and 1
  Thread-2 → partition 2
(3 partitions ÷ 2 threads: one thread gets 2 partitions)

Second instance with concurrency=2:
  Now 4 threads in group 'g1' total (2 from each instance)
  Kafka rebalances: each of the 4 threads gets... 
  1 partition, and 1 thread sits IDLE (3 partitions < 4 threads)

concurrency=5 on 3-partition topic:
  5 threads registered, but only 3 get partitions
  2 threads are idle — WASTED resources
  Rule: concurrency ≤ partition count

hints: Kafka distributes partitions evenly across consumer threads in same group
Threads > partitions = idle threads (wasted)
Each partition assigned to exactly one consumer thread per group

exercises.title: Complete consumer application.properties
exercises.scenario: Consumer needs deserializer and trusted packages configured.
exercises.starter_code:
spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers=localhost:9092

# Deserialize keys (String):
spring.kafka.consumer.key-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.____Deserializer

# Deserialize values (JSON objects):
spring.kafka.consumer.value-deserializer=org.springframework.kafka.support.serializer.____Deserializer

# Trust event packages (REQUIRED for JSON deserializer):
spring.kafka.consumer.properties.spring.json.trusted.packages=com.codequest.____

# Start from beginning when no offset exists:
spring.kafka.consumer.auto-offset-reset=____

blanks.hint: Start from beginning when no offset committed

hints: trusted.packages: without this, JsonDeserializer rejects all messages
earliest: don't miss messages published before consumer started

exercises.title: Minimal @KafkaListener
exercises.scenario: Subscribe to a topic and process each message.
exercises.instructions:
Write a `StockPriceConsumer` @Service that:
1. @KafkaListener on topic 'stock-prices', groupId='analytics-group'
2. Accepts StockPriceEvent(symbol, price, timestamp)
3. Prints: 'PRICE UPDATE: {symbol} = ₹{price}'
4. No error handling needed
hints: @KafkaListener(topics='...', groupId='...'): two key attributes
Method parameter type = deserialized event class
Spring Kafka calls this method for each message automatically

exercises.title: @KafkaListener with ConsumerRecord — access metadata
exercises.scenario: ConsumerRecord gives access to topic, partition, offset, key.
exercises.instructions:
Write `AuditConsumer` that:
1. @KafkaListener on 'trade-executed', groupId='audit-group'
2. Accepts ConsumerRecord<String, TradeExecutedEvent>
3. Logs: 'AUDIT: topic={t} partition={p} offset={o} key={k} tradeId={id}'
4. Saves the audit entry to AuditRepository
hints: ConsumerRecord<K,V>: key=String, value=TradeExecutedEvent
record.partition() and record.offset(): Kafka metadata
record.value(): the deserialized event object

exercises.title: @RetryableTopic — retry with exponential backoff
exercises.scenario: Transient failures should be retried before declaring failure.
exercises.instructions:
Write `InventoryConsumer.reserveStock(OrderPlacedEvent event)` with:
1. @KafkaListener on 'order-placed', groupId='inventory-group'
2. @RetryableTopic: 4 attempts, 500ms initial delay, multiplier=2.0
3. Method calls inventoryService.reserve() which may throw RuntimeException
4. @DltHandler: logs permanently failed orders
hints: @RetryableTopic creates intermediate retry topics automatically
attempts='4': 1 original + 3 retries = 4 total attempts
@DltHandler: no Exception parameter — receives the original event

exercises.title: Concurrent consumer — parallel processing
exercises.scenario: Multiple consumer threads process partitions in parallel.
exercises.instructions:
Write `PortfolioConsumer` with:
1. @KafkaListener on 'trade-executed', groupId='portfolio-group'
2. concurrency='3' (3 threads, for a 3-partition topic)
3. Handler calls portfolioService.updateHolding(event)
4. Log which thread processes each message
5. Note: portfolioService must be thread-safe
hints: concurrency='3': 3 listener threads created by Spring Kafka
Each thread is assigned partitions from the consumer group assignment
The @Service is SHARED — listener method called from multiple threads simultaneously

exercises.title: @EmbeddedKafka test — verify listener is called
exercises.scenario: Test that publishing a message triggers the @KafkaListener method.
exercises.instructions:
Write a @SpringBootTest test that:
1. @EmbeddedKafka with 'stock-prices' topic
2. @MockBean StockPriceService (inject into consumer)
3. Publishes a StockPriceEvent via KafkaTemplate
4. Waits for listener to process (CountDownLatch or Thread.sleep)
5. Verify StockPriceService.update() was called with correct symbol
hints: @MockBean StockPriceService: Spring injects this mock into the consumer
auto-offset-reset=earliest: consumer reads from beginning of embedded topic
trusted.packages=*: for testing only; avoids package trust configuration

exercises.title: Multiple consumer groups — same topic, independent consumption
exercises.scenario: Two services consume the same topic independently with different group IDs.
exercises.instructions:
Write two consumers on the SAME topic 'order-placed':
1. `InventoryConsumer`: groupId='inventory-group', reserves stock
2. `EmailConsumer`: groupId='email-group', sends confirmation email

Both receive EVERY message independently. Show that they have different groupIds and different behaviors.
hints: Different groupId: different offset tracking, independent consumption
Kafka delivers the same message to EACH consumer group
If email-group is slow, inventory-group is unaffected

exercises.title: Batch listener — process List of messages
exercises.scenario: Batch consumption is more efficient than one-at-a-time for high-throughput.
exercises.instructions:
Write `AnalyticsConsumer` that:
1. @KafkaListener on 'trade-executed', groupId='analytics-batch', batch='true'
2. Accepts List<TradeExecutedEvent> events
3. Logs: 'Processing batch of N events'
4. Inserts all events in one DB batch operation
5. Note: batch size depends on max.poll.records config
hints: batch='true': method receives List<EventType> instead of single EventType
max.poll.records: controls max events per batch call
Batch DB insert much faster than N individual inserts for analytics

exercises.title: What happens when @KafkaListener throws?
exercises.scenario: Unhandled exceptions in @KafkaListener trigger Kafka's error handling.
exercises.expected_solution:
Immediately: Spring Kafka's default error handler catches the exception
Default behavior: logs the error; commits the offset; message NOT retried
→ message is LOST (offset committed = Kafka thinks it was processed)

This is the default SeekToCurrentErrorHandler in older Spring Kafka,
or DefaultErrorHandler in Spring Kafka 2.8+ which retries with backoff.

To handle properly:
1. Add @RetryableTopic for automatic retry + DLT
2. OR configure DefaultErrorHandler with DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer
3. OR validate input BEFORE processing and skip invalid messages gracefully
4. Never silently lose messages in production

test_cases.expected:
Immediately: Spring Kafka's default error handler catches the exception
Default behavior: logs the error; commits the offset; message NOT retried
→ message is LOST (offset committed = Kafka thinks it was processed)

This is the default SeekToCurrentErrorHandler in older Spring Kafka,
or DefaultErrorHandler in Spring Kafka 2.8+ which retries with backoff.

To handle properly:
1. Add @RetryableTopic for automatic retry + DLT
2. OR configure DefaultErrorHandler with DeadLetterPublishingRecoverer
3. OR validate input BEFORE processing and skip invalid messages gracefully
4. Never silently lose messages in production

hints: Default behavior without @RetryableTopic: depends on Spring Kafka version
Spring Kafka 2.8+: DefaultErrorHandler retries 10 times by default
Best practice: always configure @RetryableTopic + @DltHandler explicitly

exercises.title: CountDownLatch — precise test synchronization
exercises.scenario: CountDownLatch is cleaner than Thread.sleep for async consumer tests.
exercises.instructions:
Rewrite the @EmbeddedKafka test using CountDownLatch:
1. Latch initialized with count=1
2. Consumer calls latch.countDown() when message processed
3. Test waits: latch.await(5, TimeUnit.SECONDS)
4. Assert: the await didn't timeout (returned true)
5. This avoids hardcoded Thread.sleep delays
hints: CountDownLatch(1): count=1, consumer calls countDown(), test awaits
latch.await(5, SECONDS): returns true if count reached 0 within timeout
AtomicReference<Event>: thread-safe reference for the received event

exercises.title: Manual acknowledgment — exactly-once processing
exercises.scenario: Manual ack: only commit offset when business logic fully succeeds.
exercises.instructions:
Write `PaymentConsumer.handlePayment(ConsumerRecord, Acknowledgment)` with:
1. ack-mode=MANUAL in application.properties
2. Method injects Acknowledgment parameter
3. Tries to process payment via paymentProcessor
4. On success: calls acknowledgment.acknowledge()
5. On failure: logs error, does NOT acknowledge (message redelivered!)
6. Show required properties config
hints: spring.kafka.listener.ack-mode=manual: enables manual acknowledgment
Acknowledgment parameter: injected by Spring Kafka when ack-mode=manual
NO acknowledge() on failure: Kafka redelivers the message

exercises.title: Complete notification service — producer + consumer roundtrip
exercises.scenario: End-to-end: publish a trade, consume it, verify SMS sent.
exercises.instructions:
Build a complete producer → consumer roundtrip test:
1. `TradeEventPublisher` @Service: publishes to 'trade-events-it'
2. `TradeNotificationConsumer` @Service: @KafkaListener, calls SmsService
3. @SpringBootTest @EmbeddedKafka test:
   - @MockBean SmsService
   - publishTrade() via TradeEventPublisher
   - CountDownLatch waits for consumer
   - verify(smsService).send(eq('U01'), contains('TCS'))

All assertions pass.
exercises.starter_code: // Complete producer → consumer integration test with @EmbeddedKafka
hints: CountDownLatch: consumer calls countDown; test awaits with timeout
@MockBean SmsService2: Spring injects mock into TradeNotificationConsumer2
verify(smsService).send(eq('U01'), contains('TCS')): Mockito assertion

exercises.title: Consumer with DLT — test retry exhaustion
exercises.scenario: Verify that after retries are exhausted, @DltHandler is called.
exercises.instructions:
Write a consumer with @RetryableTopic(attempts='2') and @DltHandler. Write a @SpringBootTest test that:
1. Makes the processing method always throw RuntimeException
2. Publishes a message
3. Verifies: the original method was called (1 attempt)
4. Verifies: the @DltHandler was called after retry exhaustion
5. Use CountDownLatch on both the main handler and DLT handler
hints: @RetryableTopic creates retry topics automatically; include them in @EmbeddedKafka
Two latches: one for main handler attempts, one for DLT invocation
backoff=100ms: short for tests; retry topics need to be in @EmbeddedKafka topics list

exercises.title: Complete Zerodha consumer pipeline — Topic 16.3 synthesis
exercises.scenario: Full notification and portfolio consumers with retry, DLT, and tests.
exercises.instructions:
Build the complete Zerodha consumer pipeline:
1. `TradeNotificationConsumer`: @KafkaListener + @RetryableTopic(3 attempts) + @DltHandler
2. `PortfolioUpdateConsumer`: @KafkaListener(concurrency='3') — updates portfolio holdings
3. Both on 'trade-executed', different groupIds
4. application.properties: consumer config + trusted.packages
5. @EmbeddedKafka @SpringBootTest: 2 tests — notification consumer called, portfolio consumer called

All 2 tests pass.
hints: Two consumers, same topic, different groupIds — both receive every message
@EmbeddedKafka topics: include ALL retry and DLT topics for @RetryableTopic
CountDownLatch per test: reset between tests using fresh latch each @Test
